# Linkly Link Shortener MCP Server ★ New (July 16)

Linkly is a link shortener with geo redirects, link rotators, conversion tracking, and a powerful API — now accessible through MCP for AI agent-driven link operations.

**Source:** mcpservers.org (submitted July 16, 2026)
**Category:** Marketing
**Author:** Linkly (official)
**Docs:** linklyhq.com/support/mcp-server

## Key Features

- **Link shortening:** Create branded short links programmatically
- **Geo redirects:** Route users to different destinations based on geographic location
- **Link rotators:** A/B test landing pages with automated link rotation
- **Conversion tracking:** Track clicks, conversions, and attribution
- **MCP-native:** Create, manage, and analyze links directly from your AI agent
- **API-first:** Full API access through MCP tools

## Business Relevance

Marketing operations for operators managing digital campaigns, affiliate programs, and content distribution. Use cases:
- **Campaign management:** Create and track campaign links without leaving your AI workflow
- **Geo-targeting:** Set up region-specific landing pages for international campaigns
- **A/B testing:** Rotate destination URLs to test landing page performance
- **Affiliate tracking:** Generate and track affiliate links with conversion attribution
- **Content distribution:** Shorten and brand links for social media, email, and content marketing
- **Analytics integration:** Pull link performance data into your AI agent for automated reporting

Essential for operators in marketing, growth, and ecommerce roles managing multi-channel campaigns.

## Integration with CorpusIQ

Pair with CorpusIQ's analytics connectors (GA4, Meta Ads, Google Ads) to connect link-level click data with full-funnel conversion data. Ask: "Which campaign links drove the highest-converting traffic this month?" and get answers spanning click data and on-site behavior.

## Limitations

- Linkly account required — free tier has usage limits
- Geo-redirect accuracy depends on IP geolocation (not 100% accurate)
- Conversion tracking requires pixel/script installation on destination pages
- Branded domains require separate configuration
